persona: Busy parent running a birthday party booth
one_sentence_verdict: Fun parent-operated selfie strip, but it still asks the parent to manage setup, saving, guest reset, and delivery.
top_confusions: Party Mode is prominent but Pro/native-gated.
Save / Share does not explain where photos go.
The app does not tell the parent whether auto-save is safe before handing it to guests.
top_missing_features: Physical setup guidance for a propped phone.
dealbreakers: Party Mode can stall after sharing.
Autosave can be off while the host thinks the booth is ready.

persona: Bride or wedding coordinator setting up a casual reception booth
one_sentence_verdict: Charming casual selfie app, but not trustworthy for a reception because printing, guest flow, and event setup are underpowered.
top_confusions: Print sounds real but exports a PNG.
Party Mode sounds like kiosk mode but still needs host oversight.
Wedding setup is scattered across templates, settings, and Party Setup.
dealbreakers: Monthly pricing feels wrong for a one-night event.

persona: Venue or bar owner evaluating photo-booth promo value

top_confusions: Branding appears limited to captions.
There is no event/campaign container.
Party Mode does not handle high-throughput guest lines.

one_sentence_verdict: The core idea is fun and private, but the app feels like a production tool instead of a simple party trick.
top_confusions: Home has too many choices.
Party Setup sounds required but is Pro/admin-heavy.
Edit hides the real output choices.

one_sentence_verdict: Cute private retro utility, but not fast or modern enough for a social creator app.
top_confusions: Story output is not a first-class path.
Boomerang is an export, not a capture mode.
The app feels event-oriented instead of creator-oriented.

persona: Corporate event marketer needing branded content

top_confusions: Brand-ready sounds stronger than what exists.

Guest delivery is ad hoc.

one_sentence_verdict: A charming phone backup or guest toy, not replacement software for paid booth work.

Party Mode is not full kiosk mode.
Branding is captions, not client design control.

persona: Casual App Store user comparing photo booth apps
one_sentence_verdict: Charming for a quick private strip, but not deep, polished, or obvious enough to justify monthly Pro.
top_confusions: Promised GIF/Boom/Video outputs are hidden behind Edit.
Free versus Pro is unclear.
Save / Share is ambiguous.
dealbreakers: App Store promise undersells paywall limits.

one_sentence_verdict: No-backend architecture is promising, but local persistence, sharing, deletion, and guest controls are not clear enough for kids.
top_confusions: Never stores them conflicts with local gallery storage.
Clear all scope is unclear.
Save / Share may expose photos externally.
dealbreakers: Deletion does not cover Photos/download/shared copies.

one_sentence_verdict: Fun digital booth, but not trustworthy as a print-quality, long-term keepsake workflow.
top_confusions: Print-ready means PNG only.
DPI and physical size are unclear.
Reopened gallery quality may be lower.
dealbreakers: No print metadata or PDF/lab handoff.

persona: Accessibility-sensitive low-vision user with mild motor impairment

top_confusions: The BoothBop wordmark is not obviously Home/cancel.
Outputs are hidden behind Edit.
Pro locks appear on web where purchase is disabled.

one_sentence_verdict: The core booth is usable, but Android/web users hit iOS-only subscription dead ends and unclear save behavior.
top_confusions: Install says full app but Pro says iOS only.
Locked Pro controls cannot be purchased.
Download/Gallery/Photos concepts blur together.
top_missing_features: Web/Android Pro path or no locks.

one_sentence_verdict: The free toy works, but the first minute buries fun under setup/export jargon and an event-host paywall.
top_confusions: Take Photos opens preview instead of starting.
Where are GIF, Video, and Boomerang?

30 templates may be mostly paid.
